human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for hospitality-and-travel property-system specificity, execution-processing lifecycle handling, healthcare-and-care-delivery boundary discipline, source quality, PHI handling, authorization/referral/eligibility prerequisites, clinical and payer escalation, access and change boundaries, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

review_notes: All required domain scenario families are covered: missing authorization/referral/eligibility dependency, PHI-sensitive controlled handling, and clinical or payer-interpretation escalation.
All queue-level must-pass families are covered: incomplete inputs, execution exception, unsupported completion, system dependency failure, and change-readiness gaps.
All required functional abilities for shared baseline, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and healthcare-and-care-delivery are represented and validated.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to hospitality-and-travel property-system execution support with healthcare-administrative guardrails for patient/member, payer, referral, authorization, PHI, clinical-risk, and claim-administrative contexts.

Authority sources cover public healthcare regulator guidance, public payer/provider operating guidance, recognized healthcare measure guidance, NIST incident and configuration guidance, PCI DSS payment security, DOJ lodging accessibility, FTC lodging fee disclosure, and OpenTravel interface standards.

Scenario set covers normal in-scope execution, missing facts, unsupported completion, system dependency failure, change-readiness gaps, eligibility/authorization/referral blockers, PHI handling, clinical and payer escalation, privileged access refusal, terminology, conflicting policy, and tenant adaptation.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later retained-implementation transition.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; tenant, payer, provider, property, supplier, vendor, processor, state, jurisdiction, and brand differences are handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
